issues.title: locked-ether
issues.description:
Contract locking ether found:
	Contract RentalNFT (contract.sol#6-79) has payable functions:
	 - RentalNFT.rentNFT() (contract.sol#62-69)
	But does not have a function to withdraw the ether
issues.filename:
Contract locking ether found:
	Contract RentalNFT (contract.sol
issues.recommendation: Add withdrawal function or make contract payable

issues.title: events-maths
issues.description:
RentalNFT.setRentalTerms(uint256,uint256) (contract.sol#56-59) should emit an event for: 
	- rentalPrice = price (contract.sol#57) 
	- rentalDuration = duration (contract.sol#58)
issues.recommendation: Review and apply security best practices

issues.title: missing-zero-check
issues.description:
RentalNFT.mint(address).to (contract.sol#48) lacks a zero-check on :
		- nftOwner = to (contract.sol#49)
issues.recommendation: Review and apply security best practices

issues.title: timestamp
issues.description:
RentalNFT.rentNFT() (contract.sol#62-69) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- block.timestamp < rentalEndTime (contract.sol#64)
issues.recommendation: Review and apply security best practices

issues.title: timestamp
issues.description:
RentalNFT.returnNFT() (contract.sol#72-77) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- block.timestamp < rentalEndTime (contract.sol#73)
issues.recommendation: Review and apply security best practices

issues.title: solc-version
issues.description:
Version constraint ^0.8.20 contains known severe issues (https://solidity.readthedocs.io/en/latest/bugs.html)
	- VerbatimInvalidDeduplication
	- FullInlinerNonExpressionSplitArgumentEvaluationOrder
	- MissingSideEffectsOnSelectorAccess.
It is used by:
	- ^0.8.20 (contract.sol#2)
issues.filename:

	- VerbatimInvalidDeduplication

It is used by:

issues.recommendation: Review and apply security best practices

issues.title: immutable-states
issues.description: RentalNFT.owner (contract.sol#14) should be immutable
issues.recommendation: Review and apply security best practices

issues.title: Dependence on predictable environment variable (SWC 116)
issues.description:
A control flow decision is made based on The block.timestamp environment variable.
The block.timestamp environment variable is used to determine a control flow decision. Note that the values of variables like coinbase, gaslimit, block number and timestamp are predictable and can be manipulated by a malicious miner. Also keep in mind that attackers know hashes of earlier blocks. Don't use any of those environment variables as sources of randomness and be aware that use of these variables introduces a certain level of trust into miners.
Classification: SWC-116
issues.recommendation: Avoid using block.timestamp for randomness

stage2_metadata.security_summary:
Contract Type: Custom
Classification Confidence: 90%
Access Control: role_based
Security Features: Standard
Implementation: Custom from scratch
Subtype: rental_nft

Reasoning: The contract involves renting NFTs, which is not covered by standard NFT templates like ERC721 or ERC1155. It includes specific functions and state variables for rental management, indicating custom business logic.
